section.heading: Warrant officer active-duty lists; competitive categories; number to be recommended for promotion; promotion zones

subsection.content: The Secretary concerned shall maintain for each armed force under the jurisdiction of that Secretary a single list of all warrant officers (other than warrant officers described in section 582 of this title) who are on active duty.
The Secretary concerned may establish competitive categories for promotion. Warrant officers in the same competitive category shall compete among themselves for promotion.

subsection.chapeau: Before convening a selection board under section 573 of this title, the Secretary concerned shall determine for each grade (or grade and competitive category) to be considered by the board the following:

paragraph.content: The maximum number of warrant officers to be recommended for promotion.
A promotion zone for warrant officers on the warrant officer active-duty list.

subsection.chapeau: The position of a warrant officer on the warrant officer active-duty list shall be determined as follows:

paragraph.content: Warrant officers shall be carried in the order of seniority of the grade in which they are serving on active duty.
Warrant officers serving in the same grade shall be carried in the order of their rank in that grade.

A warrant officer on the warrant officer active-duty list who receives a temporary appointment or a temporary assignment in a grade other than a warrant officer grade or chief warrant officer grade shall retain his position on the warrant officer active-duty list while so serving.

subsection.content: A chief warrant officer may not be considered for promotion to the next higher grade under this chapter until the officer has completed two years of service on active duty in the grade in which the officer is serving.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1996—Subsec. (e). Pub. L. 104–201 substituted “two years of service” for “three years of service”.
1994—Subsecs. (a), (b). Pub. L. 103–337 substituted “Secretary concerned” for “Secretary of each military department”.
1992—Subsec. (d)(3). Pub. L. 102–484 substituted “active-duty list” for “active duty list” before “while”.

note.heading: Effective Date of 1994 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 103–337 effective on the first day of the fourth month beginning after Oct. 5, 1994, see section 541(h) of Pub. L. 103–337, set out as a note under section 571 of this title.

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective Feb. 1, 1992, see section 1132 of Pub. L. 102–190, set out as an Effective Date of 1991 Amendment note under section 521 of this title.
